privacy_notice: Contains highly sensitive personal, medical, disability, trauma, and identity information. Store privately, share only with William's consent, and avoid using it to make assumptions beyond this profile.
medical_safety_note: This JSON is a support-planning and accessibility profile assembled from user-supplied information plus selected uploaded life-impact JSON entries. It is not a diagnosis, treatment plan, emergency plan, or substitute for medical/mental-health care.

source_archive.selection_method: Matched only uploaded JSON entries relevant to the user-supplied William/T-Rex/Dino list, then added user-supplied items that were not present in the base JSONs.

language_guidance: Do not describe William as nonbinary or feminine.
Do not reduce William to his diagnoses, trauma history, body size, mobility aids, or assigned sex at birth.

care_context.caregiver_role_summary: William's fiancé helps with daily care, mobility/access support, and practical tasks. The caregiver's capacity should be respected and pacing/back-up planning may be needed.
care_context.communication_preference: Trauma-informed, direct, validating, non-shaming, autonomy-preserving language.

mobility_aids_adaptive_equipment_and_dai.name: Shower chair
life_limitations_addressed: Bathing while standing may be unsafe, exhausting, painful, or impossible during weakness, vertigo, MS/EDS flares, back pain, asthma symptoms, or severe fatigue.
Bathroom tasks can carry high fall risk because of wet surfaces, transfers, dizziness, and limited stamina.
possible_life_improvements_or_supports: Can make bathing more possible while seated.
Can reduce fall risk and reduce spoon cost of hygiene.
Can allow pacing, partial bathing, or caregiver-assisted bathing with more safety and dignity.
accessibility_notes: Needs stable placement, reachable supplies, non-slip setup, transfer planning, and enough space for the power wheelchair or caregiver assistance.

mobility_aids_adaptive_equipment_and_dai.name: Power wheelchair
life_limitations_addressed: Barely being able to walk/being mostly bedbound can make distance, errands, appointments, standing lines, transfers, and basic movement unsafe or impossible without powered mobility.
Manual propulsion may be unrealistic because of extreme short arms, back injury, EDS pain, MS fatigue/weakness, respiratory limitations, and limited stamina.
possible_life_improvements_or_supports: Can conserve energy by making movement less physically expensive.
Can reduce fall risk and increase access to appointments, home spaces, stores, and social participation.
Can provide independence when walking or manual wheelchair use is not reliable.
accessibility_notes: Needs charging routine, maintenance, accessible route planning, turning-radius checks, transfer planning, and backup options during equipment failure.

mobility_aids_adaptive_equipment_and_dai.name: Oxygen
life_limitations_addressed: Severe asthma/respiratory impairment can affect stamina, sleep, speaking, showering, transfers, walking, and safe recovery from exertion or infection.
Oxygen equipment can add tubing, tank/concentrator logistics, power needs, travel planning, stigma, and safety precautions.
possible_life_improvements_or_supports: Can support breathing and daily functioning when prescribed/needed.
Can make pacing and recovery safer when coordinated with medical instructions.
Can reduce fear when respiratory symptoms are unpredictable, if an emergency plan is clear.
accessibility_notes: Needs tubing management, trip-hazard reduction, equipment access near bed/chair/bathroom, emergency instructions, and fire/smoke safety.

mobility_aids_adaptive_equipment_and_dai.name: Fiancé as caregiver
life_limitations_addressed: Bedbound/barely-walking status and complex chronic illness can make daily activities difficult to complete alone.
Frequent hospitalizations, mobility limits, toileting/bathing barriers, oxygen needs, and cognitive/energy crashes can require practical and emotional support.
possible_life_improvements_or_supports: Consistent caregiver support can improve safety, reduce missed needs, preserve energy, support emotional regulation, and make home routines more possible.
Clear task lists and boundaries can reduce stress for both William and the caregiver.
accessibility_notes: Care routines should be split into small steps, include rest breaks, and avoid assuming unlimited caregiver capacity.

mobility_aids_adaptive_equipment_and_dai.name: Bidet
life_limitations_addressed: Toileting hygiene may be limited by back injury, extreme short arms, EDS pain, mobility limits, body size, fatigue, vertigo, and difficulty twisting/reaching.
Relying on caregiver help for intimate hygiene can affect privacy, dignity, and trauma safety.
possible_life_improvements_or_supports: Can increase independence and dignity during toileting.
Can reduce painful reaching/twisting and reduce dependence on caregiver help for hygiene.
accessibility_notes: Needs reachable controls, safe transfer setup, cleaning plan, and compatibility with William's bathroom arrangement.

mobility_aids_adaptive_equipment_and_dai.name: Extender tool for toileting
life_limitations_addressed: Short arm reach, back injury, body size, EDS instability, pain, and limited mobility can make wiping/cleaning physically difficult or unsafe.
Toileting assistance can be emotionally vulnerable, especially with trauma history and gender dysphoria.
possible_life_improvements_or_supports: Can improve independence, privacy, and confidence with toileting.
accessibility_notes: Needs a discreet storage place, cleaning routine, and backup plan for flare days.

user_reported_health_disability_and_neur.name: Multiple Sclerosis
william_specific_context: Part of William's complex chronic illness profile.
Interacts with power wheelchair use, oxygen/respiratory strain, fatigue, vertigo, seizure hospitalizations, infection vulnerability, and limited walking.
likely_life_impacts_to_account_for: Unpredictable fatigue, weakness, numbness/tingling, pain, coordination issues, balance problems, possible cognitive fog, and variable flare days.
May make walking, transfers, bathing, errands, cooking, appointments, and social plans unreliable.
Heat, infections, stress, poor sleep, or overexertion may worsen functioning.
support_design_notes: Use pacing, rest breaks, accessible routes, seated options, appointment reminders, symptom tracking, and flexible expectations.
Avoid interpreting inconsistency as unwillingness.

user_reported_health_disability_and_neur.name: PCOS / PMOS
user_reported_health_disability_and_neur.terminology_note: User supplied: 'Polycystic Ovary Syndrome has officially been renamed Polyendocrine Metabolic Ovarian Syndrome.' This profile preserves both PCOS and PMOS language as provided by William.
william_specific_context: May intersect with gender dysphoria because reproductive/gynecologic care can be misgendering or body-dysphoria-triggering for a binary trans man.
May interact with morbid obesity, metabolic concerns, fatigue, pain, and endocrine symptoms.
likely_life_impacts_to_account_for: Hormonal/metabolic symptoms can affect weight, energy, pain, mood, bleeding/cycles if present, skin/hair changes, insulin resistance risk, and medical appointments.
Care may require extra privacy, consent, correct gendering, and avoidance of language that feminizes William.
support_design_notes: Use neutral/anatomical or William-approved language for body-specific care.
Offer reminders and tracking without shaming body size, food, hormones, fertility, or gender.

user_reported_health_disability_and_neur.name: Bedridden / barely can walk
william_specific_context: William is mostly bedbound and barely able to walk.
This should be treated as a central access need, not as laziness, avoidance, or lack of willpower.
likely_life_impacts_to_account_for: High spoon cost for transfers, toileting, showering, dressing, leaving home, cooking, cleaning, and appointments.
Increased risk from falls, pressure discomfort/injury, deconditioning, isolation, pain flares, and emergency access barriers.
support_design_notes: Default to bed-level, seated, or wheelchair-accessible task options.
Split tasks into micro-steps and reward partial completion.
Build in recovery time and caregiver backup options.

user_reported_health_disability_and_neur.name: Ehlers-Danlos syndrome, hypermobile type
william_specific_context: Part of William's mobility, pain, bathroom, showering, and reach-access profile.
Interacts with back injury, short arms, power wheelchair use, and toileting/shower adaptations.
likely_life_impacts_to_account_for: Can cause joint instability, subluxations/dislocations, widespread pain, fatigue, soft-tissue injury, and difficulty with repetitive or forceful tasks.
May make reaching, bending, twisting, transfers, standing, and showering painful or unsafe.
support_design_notes: Use joint-protection strategies, seated tasks, adaptive tools, reduced reaching, careful transfers, and gentle pacing.
Avoid pushing through pain as a default.

user_reported_health_disability_and_neur.name: Hypothyroidism
william_specific_context: Interacts with fatigue, brain fog, mood, weight/metabolic concerns, cold intolerance, sleep disruption, and overall spoon budget.
likely_life_impacts_to_account_for: May affect energy, cognition, mood, body temperature, weight, skin/hair, sleep, and daily reliability.
Can make task initiation and follow-through harder when symptoms are undertreated or fluctuating.
support_design_notes: Use medication/routine reminders if wanted, flexible scheduling, low-energy task options, and non-shaming check-ins.

user_reported_health_disability_and_neur.name: Broken back from being struck/flung across an intersection as a pedestrian in 2011, with repeated re-injury
user_reported_health_disability_and_neur.category: traumatic physical injury / chronic pain / mobility
william_specific_context: A major traumatic injury from 2011 with repeated re-injury.
May create both physical limitations and trauma triggers around traffic, intersections, pedestrians, pain, loss of mobility, and medical systems.
likely_life_impacts_to_account_for: Can limit walking, standing, sitting, transfers, lifting, bending, reaching, bathing, toileting, sleep, transportation, and appointments.
Repeated re-injury may make symptom flares unpredictable and recovery non-linear.
support_design_notes: Use no-lift/no-bend task alternatives, reachable supplies, transfer safety, seated routines, pain pacing, and trauma-informed language.

user_reported_health_disability_and_neur.name: Borderline Personality Disorder: Discouraged, Petulant, and Self-Destructive patterns
user_reported_health_disability_and_neur.category: mental health / emotion regulation / attachment trauma
william_specific_context: William identifies Discouraged, Petulant, and Self-Destructive BPD patterns.
Should be supported without stigma, villainizing, or assuming manipulation.
likely_life_impacts_to_account_for: May include intense fear of abandonment, shame, anger, emotional swings, dependence needs, self-hatred, rejection sensitivity, impulsivity, relationship insecurity, and crisis vulnerability.
Discouraged pattern may look quiet, passive, clingy, withdrawn, self-blaming, or intensely afraid of rejection.
Petulant pattern may involve anger, dissatisfaction, possessiveness, control fears, and feeling unworthy or unseen.
Self-destructive pattern may involve self-hatred, bitterness, risky behavior, or self-harm urges.
support_design_notes: Use DBT-informed validation, clear reassurance, predictable follow-through, gentle boundaries, repair after conflict, and non-shaming crisis planning.
Avoid silent treatment, abandonment threats, baiting, moralizing, or dismissing emotions as 'too much'.

user_reported_health_disability_and_neur.name: Reactive Attachment Disorder / attachment trauma
william_specific_context: Interacts with foster care abuse, repeated abandonment/loss, abusive caregivers/parents, relationship trauma, and loss of support systems.
likely_life_impacts_to_account_for: May affect trust, closeness, fear of rejection, emotional safety, conflict response, help-seeking, and ability to believe support will stay.
Can make inconsistency, sudden changes, unclear tone, or perceived abandonment feel dangerous.
support_design_notes: Use consistency, transparency, reassurance, consent, repair language, and clear expectations.
Do not punish bids for reassurance; guide them into safe, sustainable requests.

user_reported_health_disability_and_neur.name: C-PTSD / complex trauma
william_specific_context: Multiple long-term trauma exposures: childhood abuse, foster care abuse, trafficking/sexual assault, hate crime, stalking/doxxing, abusive partners, repeated losses, homelessness, and medical trauma.
likely_life_impacts_to_account_for: May involve hypervigilance, nightmares, flashbacks, dissociation, shame, avoidance, startle response, mistrust, emotional flooding, and difficulty feeling safe.
Can worsen pain, fatigue, asthma/breathing distress, sleep, executive function, and hospital stress.
support_design_notes: Use grounding options, consent, predictable steps, privacy protections, emotional validation, and gentle choices.
Avoid surprise demands, coercion, graphic reminders, blame, or forced disclosure.

user_reported_health_disability_and_neur.name: Narcolepsy
william_specific_context: Interacts with fatigue, bedbound status, driving/transportation safety, cooking/showering risk, medication routines, and daily scheduling.
likely_life_impacts_to_account_for: Can cause sudden sleepiness, sleep attacks, disrupted sleep/wake cycles, brain fog, and safety risks during transfers, bathing, cooking, or travel.
May be misunderstood as laziness or avoidance.
support_design_notes: Use flexible scheduling, nap/recovery blocks, alarms/reminders, safety checks for risky tasks, and non-shaming language.

user_reported_health_disability_and_neur.name: Lymphedema
william_specific_context: May interact with morbid obesity, mobility limits, pain, recurrent infections/hospitalizations, and wheelchair/bed positioning.
likely_life_impacts_to_account_for: Can cause swelling, heaviness, pain, skin tightness, difficulty with clothes/shoes/transfers, reduced mobility, and increased infection concern.
May make standing/walking, showering, and wound/skin monitoring harder.
support_design_notes: Use skin checks, gentle positioning, accessible clothing, pacing, and clinician-approved compression/elevation routines if applicable.

user_reported_health_disability_and_neur.name: Severe asthma
william_specific_context: Interacts with oxygen use, infections, fatigue, showering, transfers, anxiety, and hospitalizations.
likely_life_impacts_to_account_for: Can limit exertion, speaking, bathing, walking, household chores, exposure to fragrances/smoke/dust/cold air, and safe recovery from illness.
Breathing distress may escalate quickly and can be frightening.
support_design_notes: Keep rescue/emergency plan accessible, reduce respiratory triggers, build rest breaks into tasks, and keep supplies within reach.

user_reported_health_disability_and_neur.name: Vertigo
william_specific_context: Interacts with showering, transfers, walking, power wheelchair navigation, bed-to-chair movement, and seizure/fall safety.
likely_life_impacts_to_account_for: Can cause dizziness, spinning sensation, nausea, disorientation, falls, and avoidance of movement.
May make bathroom and transfer tasks high-risk.
support_design_notes: Use seated options, slow transitions, grab bars/supports when available, clear floors, and caregiver standby for risky tasks.

user_reported_health_disability_and_neur.name: Frequent infection and seizure hospitalizations
william_specific_context: Frequent hospitalizations can increase medical trauma, disrupt routines, drain spoons, and create fear around symptoms.
likely_life_impacts_to_account_for: May require emergency information, medication lists, hospital bag planning, seizure/infection warning-sign tracking, and caregiver communication.
Recovery days after hospitalization may require reduced expectations and extra emotional support.
support_design_notes: Use an emergency profile, calm checklists, post-hospital rest plan, and clear thresholds for when to seek urgent help as directed by clinicians.

user_reported_health_disability_and_neur.name: Gender dysphoria as a binary trans man
user_reported_health_disability_and_neur.category: identity / trauma-informed body care / healthcare access
william_specific_context: William is strictly binary male, legally male, assigned female at birth, pre-surgery, and has facial hair.
Gendered body care, bathroom help, reproductive/gynecologic topics, and medical paperwork can be dysphoria-triggering if misgendered or feminized.
likely_life_impacts_to_account_for: Can affect bathing, toileting assistance, medical care, clothing, body talk, intimacy, documentation, and willingness to seek care.
Misgendering can worsen anxiety, shame, anger, avoidance, and trauma responses.
support_design_notes: Use male language, correct name/identity, privacy, consent, and William-approved anatomy language.
Never frame care needs as making him less of a man.

user_reported_health_disability_and_neur.name: Morbid obesity
user_reported_health_disability_and_neur.category: body size / mobility / stigma / equipment access
william_specific_context: Interacts with mobility, oxygen/asthma, lymphedema, PCOS/PMOS, back injury, power wheelchair needs, shower chair sizing, toileting tools, and medical stigma.
likely_life_impacts_to_account_for: Can affect equipment fit, safe transfers, bathroom access, seating, transportation, respiratory effort, pain, and clinician bias.
Weight stigma can create avoidance, shame, delayed care, and mistrust.
support_design_notes: Use size-inclusive equipment, non-shaming language, practical access planning, and focus on safety/function rather than blame.

user_reported_health_disability_and_neur.name: Severe ADHD
william_specific_context: Interacts with anxiety, autism, trauma, narcolepsy, chronic pain, medical routines, task initiation, and overwhelm.
likely_life_impacts_to_account_for: Can affect memory, initiation, prioritizing, time awareness, emotional regulation, follow-through, organization, and switching tasks.
Medical and accessibility tasks can be especially hard when urgent, boring, or emotionally loaded.
support_design_notes: Use tiny steps, timers, visible checklists, rewards, body-doubling, low-demand restarts, and no-shame reminders.

user_reported_health_disability_and_neur.name: Autism Spectrum Disorder
william_specific_context: Interacts with trauma, anxiety, sensory overload, medical settings, communication needs, routine disruptions, and social misunderstandings.
likely_life_impacts_to_account_for: May affect sensory tolerance, transitions, communication, social energy, routines, food/clothing tolerance, and overload recovery.
Stress, pain, hospitals, and conflict may reduce communication capacity.
support_design_notes: Use clear language, predictable routines, sensory-friendly options, low-pressure choices, and time to process.

user_reported_health_disability_and_neur.name: Anxiety, stress, imposter syndrome, and people-pleasing
william_specific_context: Interacts with BPD/RAD/C-PTSD, repeated losses, abusive relationships, medical complexity, and caregiver dynamics.
likely_life_impacts_to_account_for: Can make William over-apologize, over-explain, minimize needs, fear being a burden, accept unsafe situations, or push past limits to avoid disappointing others.
Can worsen shutdowns, anger, panic, dysphoria, pain, and fatigue.
support_design_notes: Use reassurance that needs are real, permission to rest, consent-based care, boundary scripts, and gentle reminders that accommodations are not failure.

user_reported_health_disability_and_neur.name: Extremely short arms / T-Rex nickname
user_reported_health_disability_and_neur.category: body mechanics / reach access / adaptive tools
william_specific_context: William's nickname T-Rex relates to extremely short arms.
This directly affects reaching, toileting, dressing, bathing, transfers, wheelchair propulsion/control setup, grabbing objects, and using standard home layouts.
likely_life_impacts_to_account_for: Can make ordinary items functionally out of reach even when they seem nearby to other people.
Can increase dependence on extender tools, caregiver help, adaptive storage, reachable controls, and bed/chair-side organization.
support_design_notes: Keep essentials within William-specific reach, use reachers/extenders, avoid high/low storage, and treat reach barriers as access issues, not inconvenience.

trauma_loss_and_safety_history_user_supp.name: Loss of major support systems
care_implications: May intensify abandonment fear, grief, distrust, and panic when support feels unstable.
Consistency, repair, and clear communication are especially important.

trauma_loss_and_safety_history_user_supp.name: Foster care: six months in a placement center with abuse
care_implications: May create intense triggers around institutions, being controlled, being trapped, staff-like authority, and not being believed.
Support should emphasize choice, consent, and present-day safety.

trauma_loss_and_safety_history_user_supp.name: Victim of hate crime: shot with pest-control pellet/BB gun, multiple entry points, right leg fracture, lead pellet still embedded in bone, international news exposure
care_implications: Can affect physical pain, leg function, trust, public safety feelings, medical trauma, and fear of being targeted.
International news exposure can intensify privacy needs and loss of control over personal story.

trauma_loss_and_safety_history_user_supp.name: Doxxing and stalking by a hate group
care_implications: Creates high privacy and safety needs.
Avoid exposing identifying information, location patterns, documents, or contact details without explicit consent.

trauma_loss_and_safety_history_user_supp.name: Severely abusive first husband; perpetrator of human trafficking
care_implications: May create triggers around control, coercion, money, sex, threats, dependence, caregiving, and being disbelieved.
Support should avoid coercive language and emphasize William's control over decisions.

trauma_loss_and_safety_history_user_supp.name: Emotionally/mentally abusive second husband; William reported him to police after finding child sexual abuse material on his devices; arrested; no contact since 2023; still legally married
care_implications: Can create grief, disgust, betrayal trauma, legal stress, and relationship safety concerns.
Use language that affirms William was not together with him when he discovered the abuse and that reporting was protective action.

trauma_loss_and_safety_history_user_supp.name: Several failed engagements
care_implications: May intensify fears of abandonment, failure, instability, and being hard to love.
Support should separate relationship outcomes from William's worth.

trauma_loss_and_safety_history_user_supp.name: Death of first love in 2007 after forced estrangement; both were 15; she developed skin cancer
care_implications: May carry grief, unresolved goodbye, forced separation trauma, and adolescent loss.
Anniversaries and reminders may affect mood and functioning.

trauma_loss_and_safety_history_user_supp.name: Miscarriages, including one pregnancy to 17 weeks; baby named Andrea Marjorie Canon
care_implications: Pregnancy loss may involve grief, body trauma, medical trauma, anniversary pain, and dysphoria-sensitive grief.
Use Andrea Marjorie Canon's name respectfully if William wants that.

trauma_loss_and_safety_history_user_supp.name: Death of second love by suicide in 2020
care_implications: May trigger grief, guilt, abandonment pain, trauma anniversaries, and fear of losing loved ones.

trauma_loss_and_safety_history_user_supp.name: Death of daughter's father by suicide in 2022
care_implications: May involve grief, parenting-related grief, trauma, anger, and fear around suicide loss.

trauma_loss_and_safety_history_user_supp.name: Death of father after estrangement in 2024
care_implications: May involve complicated grief, unresolved abuse/estrangement feelings, anger, relief, guilt, and family-system triggers.

trauma_loss_and_safety_history_user_supp.name: Sexual assault survivor and human trafficking survivor
care_implications: Body care, bathing, toileting, medical exams, intimate relationships, and caregiver assistance must be consent-forward and privacy-protective.
Avoid coercive framing, surprise touch, shaming, or graphic language.

trauma_loss_and_safety_history_user_supp.name: Past homelessness on streets, couch/floor surfing, shelters, and repeated loss of homes
care_implications: May create scarcity fear, attachment to possessions/home security, panic around instability, and difficulty trusting safety.
Support should respect the emotional importance of stable shelter and belongings.

trauma_loss_and_safety_history_user_supp.name: All four parents abusive in all ways except sexual; stepfather was sexually abusive
care_implications: May create complex family trauma, authority triggers, distrust, shame, and difficulty accepting care.
Family references should not assume parents are safe or supportive.

trauma_loss_and_safety_history_user_supp.name: Sibling favoritism
care_implications: May create sensitivity to comparison, exclusion, being less valued, or being replaced.
Avoid comparing William's needs, grief, progress, or disability to siblings/others.

mobility: Offer bed-level and seated options before standing/walking options.
Avoid stairs, long distances, tight spaces, and tasks requiring bending/lifting/reaching unless explicitly marked accessible.
bathroom_and_hygiene: Use shower chair, bidet, toileting extender, reachable supplies, and privacy-respecting caregiver assistance when needed.
Treat toileting/bathing as dignity-sensitive and trauma-sensitive.
Use male language and William-approved anatomy language.
respiratory: Account for oxygen access, asthma triggers, infection recovery, exertion limits, and emergency planning.
Reduce smoke, strong fragrance, dust, cold-air exposure, and rushed exertion when possible.
energy_and_spoons: Split every care task into tiny steps.
Count partial completion as real progress.
Build rest and recovery into the plan after bathing, toileting, transfers, appointments, emotional conversations, and hospitalizations.
neurodivergence_and_executive_function: Use clear steps, visual lists, reminders, timers, body-doubling, predictable routines, and low-demand restarts.
Avoid shame for forgetfulness, task paralysis, overwhelm, or inconsistent capacity.
trauma_and_attachment: Use consent, validation, consistency, repair, and explicit reassurance.
Avoid threats of abandonment, silent treatment, moralizing, coercion, and surprise demands.
Protect privacy because of doxxing/stalking history.
body_size_and_reach: Use size-inclusive equipment and room layouts.
Keep essentials within William's actual arm reach from bed and wheelchair.
Use adaptive tools without jokes unless William initiates humor.

tone: Warm, validating, direct, masculine-affirming, disability-affirming, and non-shaming.
William is not lazy, dramatic, manipulative, or failing because his capacity changes.
task_system: Every task should have micro-steps, optional caregiver support flags, and a low-spoon version.
Examples: 'sit up', 'take inhaler/oxygen check if applicable', 'transfer safely', 'gather supplies', 'wash one body area', 'rest', 'continue if able'.
Reward effort and safety, not just task completion.
caregiver_system: Let caregiver tasks show William's access needs while also honoring caregiver limits.
Include backup plans and 'not today, try smaller' options.
crisis_or_escalation_language: Use grounding and support prompts, but do not replace emergency/clinical care.
For seizure, respiratory distress, severe infection signs, suicidal crisis, or acute safety threats, follow William's real emergency plan and local emergency services.
privacy: Do not expose legal name, location, medical details, trauma details, or contact information in public-facing UI.
gender_affirming_care: Avoid feminizing reproductive or anatomy-related care language.
Give privacy and control during bathing, toileting, and medical reminders.

items_added_from_user_prompt_without_dir: PCOS / PMOS terminology and William-specific gender-dysphoria interaction
Broken back from 2011 pedestrian impact and repeated re-injury
Hate crime with pellet/BB gun injuries and embedded lead pellet
Doxxing and stalking by hate group
Abusive second husband / CSAM report / no contact since 2023
Death of second love by suicide in 2020
Death of daughter's father by suicide in 2022
Death of father after estrangement in 2024
Sexual assault and human trafficking survivorship
Binary trans male specifics: legally male, AFAB, pre-surgery, facial hair
Past homelessness and repeated loss of homes
Abusive parents and sexually abusive stepfather

source_entries_used.name: Mobility aid/device
source_entries_used.source_pdf: Your Complete Mobility Guide_ A Simple Guide to Mobility Aids _ Southern.pdf
imported_life_limitations: May require learning, fit/measurement, maintenance, storage, transportation space, accessible routes, and social confidence to use well.
Can be limited by stairs, narrow doorways, uneven ground, snow/ice, inaccessible bathrooms, crowded spaces, or stigma.
imported_possible_life_improvements_or_s: Can improve safety, independence, comfort, confidence, and participation by reducing falls, pain, fatigue, or distance barriers.
Can conserve spoons by making movement less physically expensive.
source_entries_used.imported_spoon_energy_notes: Usually reduces mobility spoon cost when matched well; may add setup/transportation spoon cost.
source_entries_used.imported_individual_variability_note: Impact can be mild, moderate, severe, intermittent, progressive, or invisible depending on the person and context.

source_entries_used.name: Power Wheelchair
source_entries_used.source_pdf: Your Complete Mobility Guide_ A Simple Guide to Mobility Aids _ Southern.pdf
imported_life_limitations: May require learning, fit/measurement, maintenance, storage, transportation space, accessible routes, and social confidence to use well.
Can be limited by stairs, narrow doorways, uneven ground, snow/ice, inaccessible bathrooms, crowded spaces, or stigma.
Requires battery charging, transport planning, enough space to turn, maintenance, and safe controls; some drive types trade obstacle climbing for tight turns or comfort.
imported_possible_life_improvements_or_s: Can improve safety, independence, comfort, confidence, and participation by reducing falls, pain, fatigue, or distance barriers.
Can conserve spoons by making movement less physically expensive.
Can provide movement without manual propulsion and may offer power seating/custom controls for complex needs.
source_entries_used.imported_spoon_energy_notes: Usually reduces mobility spoon cost when matched well; may add setup/transportation spoon cost.
source_entries_used.imported_individual_variability_note: Impact can be mild, moderate, severe, intermittent, progressive, or invisible depending on the person and context.

source_entries_used.name: Manual Wheelchair
source_entries_used.source_pdf: Your Complete Mobility Guide_ A Simple Guide to Mobility Aids _ Southern.pdf
imported_life_limitations: May require learning, fit/measurement, maintenance, storage, transportation space, accessible routes, and social confidence to use well.
Can be limited by stairs, narrow doorways, uneven ground, snow/ice, inaccessible bathrooms, crowded spaces, or stigma.
May require upper-body strength or another person for propulsion depending on chair/person; inaccessible spaces and transport can be major barriers.
imported_possible_life_improvements_or_s: Can improve safety, independence, comfort, confidence, and participation by reducing falls, pain, fatigue, or distance barriers.
Can conserve spoons by making movement less physically expensive.
Can make indoor/outdoor movement possible with reduced walking/standing demands and may support everyday activities despite reduced mobility.
source_entries_used.imported_spoon_energy_notes: Usually reduces mobility spoon cost when matched well; may add setup/transportation spoon cost.
source_entries_used.imported_individual_variability_note: Impact can be mild, moderate, severe, intermittent, progressive, or invisible depending on the person and context.

source_entries_used.name: Toileting/Grooming Issue
source_entries_used.source_pdf: A to Z of Disabilities and Accommodations.pdf
imported_life_limitations: Can limit the specific daily/work function named here: Toileting/Grooming Issue.
May make tasks slower, less safe, more painful, more tiring, or less reliable unless the environment is adjusted.
Can interact with other conditions, so a person may need supports that address multiple limitations at once.
imported_possible_life_improvements_or_s: Naming the limitation can make accommodation conversations more practical because support can focus on the actual barrier instead of only the diagnosis.
Assistive technology, altered timing, task redesign, or environmental changes can slightly improve independence and reduce exhaustion.
source_entries_used.imported_spoon_energy_notes: Spoon cost rises when the task repeatedly demands this function without breaks or tools.
source_entries_used.imported_individual_variability_note: Impact can be mild, moderate, severe, intermittent, progressive, or invisible depending on the person and context.

source_entries_used.name: Sitting
source_entries_used.source_pdf: A to Z of Disabilities and Accommodations.pdf
imported_life_limitations: Can limit the specific daily/work function named here: Sitting.
May make tasks slower, less safe, more painful, more tiring, or less reliable unless the environment is adjusted.
Can interact with other conditions, so a person may need supports that address multiple limitations at once.
imported_possible_life_improvements_or_s: Naming the limitation can make accommodation conversations more practical because support can focus on the actual barrier instead of only the diagnosis.
Assistive technology, altered timing, task redesign, or environmental changes can slightly improve independence and reduce exhaustion.
source_entries_used.imported_spoon_energy_notes: Spoon cost rises when the task repeatedly demands this function without breaks or tools.
source_entries_used.imported_individual_variability_note: Impact can be mild, moderate, severe, intermittent, progressive, or invisible depending on the person and context.

source_entries_used.name: Respiratory Impairments
source_entries_used.source_pdf: A to Z of Disabilities and Accommodations.pdf
imported_life_limitations: May limit stamina, pain tolerance, immune safety, digestion, breathing, sleep, temperature tolerance, medical scheduling, or physical reliability.
Can make work/school, errands, self-care, food planning, socializing, and travel harder due to symptoms, flares, treatment, or environmental triggers.
Invisible symptoms may be misunderstood, causing pressure to overexert.
imported_possible_life_improvements_or_s: Medical recognition and accommodations can support safer pacing, trigger reduction, treatment access, and clearer expectations.
Adaptive routines can slightly improve autonomy and reduce flares or crashes.
source_entries_used.imported_spoon_energy_notes: Spoon cost often fluctuates with flares, treatment side effects, pain, fatigue, weather, food, sleep, and stress.
source_entries_used.imported_individual_variability_note: Impact can be mild, moderate, severe, intermittent, progressive, or invisible depending on the person and context.

source_entries_used.imported_individual_variability_note: Impact can be mild, moderate, severe, intermittent, progressive, or invisible depending on the person and context.

source_entries_used.name: Multiple sclerosis (MS)
source_entries_used.source_pdf: Autoimmune Diseases_ Types, Symptoms & Treatments.pdf
imported_life_limitations: Autoimmune disease can be chronic and may require lifelong symptom management, flare planning, appointments, and treatment decisions.
Symptoms may come and go, which can make work, chores, caregiving, social plans, and mobility less predictable.
Can limit walking, balance, vision, strength, breathing/swallowing in serious cases, heat tolerance, endurance, fine motor tasks, and independence.
imported_possible_life_improvements_or_s: A clear diagnosis can support treatment, flare tracking, accommodations, and self-advocacy.
Recognizing triggers and early warning signs can slightly improve pacing and reduce preventable crashes.
Mobility aids, therapy, cooling/rest strategies, and safety planning can improve participation and reduce falls or overexertion.
source_entries_used.imported_spoon_energy_notes: Spoons may fluctuate with inflammation, pain, fatigue, treatment side effects, infection risk, and flares.
source_entries_used.imported_individual_variability_note: Impact can be mild, moderate, severe, intermittent, progressive, or invisible depending on the person and context.

source_entries_used.source_pdf: A to Z of Disabilities and Accommodations.pdf
imported_life_limitations: May limit stamina, pain tolerance, immune safety, digestion, breathing, sleep, temperature tolerance, medical scheduling, or physical reliability.

source_entries_used.imported_individual_variability_note: Impact can be mild, moderate, severe, intermittent, progressive, or invisible depending on the person and context.

source_entries_used.name: Hypothyroidism
source_entries_used.source_pdf: Types of Endocrine Disorders _ UI Health.pdf
imported_life_limitations: Hormone imbalance can affect multiple body systems, so symptoms may interfere with energy, mood, body temperature, appetite/weight, sleep, concentration, strength, blood pressure, and daily reliability.
May limit energy, warmth, mood, focus, digestion, weight regulation, heart rate, skin/hair comfort, and motivation through slowed body processes.
imported_possible_life_improvements_or_s: Diagnosis and monitoring can slightly improve life by making symptoms explainable, treatable, and easier to plan around.
Correct thyroid replacement and routine tracking can restore more predictable energy for many people.
source_entries_used.imported_spoon_energy_notes: Spoon cost depends on whether hormones are stable, undertreated, overtreated, or affected by illness/stress.
source_entries_used.imported_individual_variability_note: Impact can be mild, moderate, severe, intermittent, progressive, or invisible depending on the person and context.

source_entries_used.name: Thyroid Disorders

source_entries_used.imported_individual_variability_note: Impact can be mild, moderate, severe, intermittent, progressive, or invisible depending on the person and context.

source_entries_used.name: Back Impairment
source_entries_used.source_pdf: A to Z of Disabilities and Accommodations.pdf
imported_life_limitations: May limit walking, standing, lifting, reaching, transfers, balance, speed, endurance, pain control, and access to inaccessible spaces.
Can make errands, bathing, cooking, transportation, cleaning, work sites, and social activities dependent on ramps, seating, rest, or assistance.
Fall risk, pain flares, and inaccessible buildings can reduce independence even when the person has strong coping skills.
imported_possible_life_improvements_or_s: Mobility aids, ergonomic tools, accessible layouts, and pacing can improve safety, range, and dignity.
Planning around energy, pain, and access can slightly increase participation and reduce injury.
source_entries_used.imported_spoon_energy_notes: Physical spoon cost rises with distance, stairs, carrying, transfers, and repeated movements.
source_entries_used.imported_individual_variability_note: Impact can be mild, moderate, severe, intermittent, progressive, or invisible depending on the person and context.

source_entries_used.name: Borderline Personality Disorder
source_entries_used.source_pdf: Alphabetical Listing of Mental Disorders.pdf
imported_life_limitations: Can affect emotion regulation, identity, trust, boundaries, attachment, impulse control, conflict, and relationship stability.
May lead to intense rejection sensitivity, withdrawal, anger, control struggles, dependency, isolation, or repeated interpersonal crises depending on the pattern.
Daily functioning can be limited by the energy spent managing feelings, perceived threat, shame, or social consequences.
imported_possible_life_improvements_or_s: Recognition can support skills-based treatment, clearer boundaries, repair conversations, and less self-blame.
Consistent routines, validation, and agreed coping steps can slightly improve relationship safety and self-control.
source_entries_used.imported_spoon_energy_notes: Spoon cost spikes during conflict, abandonment fear, criticism, shame, or interpersonal uncertainty.
source_entries_used.imported_individual_variability_note: Impact can be mild, moderate, severe, intermittent, progressive, or invisible depending on the person and context.

source_entries_used.name: Post Traumatic Stress Disorder
source_entries_used.source_pdf: Alphabetical Listing of Mental Disorders.pdf
imported_life_limitations: Can shrink the person’s world through avoidance, panic, hypervigilance, intrusive worry, and fear of places/situations where escape or safety feels uncertain.
May limit appointments, errands, work/school, social connection, sleep, concentration, travel, conflict resolution, and asking for help.
The person may appear “fine” while spending a large amount of internal energy monitoring threat or preventing a panic/trauma response.
imported_possible_life_improvements_or_s: Recognizing the condition can support grounding skills, exposure at a tolerable pace, trauma-informed care, and clearer boundaries.
Predictable routines, safety plans, and permission to pause can slightly improve autonomy and reduce shame.
source_entries_used.imported_spoon_energy_notes: Variable spoon cost; even short tasks may become expensive when they involve crowds, uncertainty, conflict, trauma cues, or sensory overload.
source_entries_used.imported_individual_variability_note: Impact can be mild, moderate, severe, intermittent, progressive, or invisible depending on the person and context.

source_entries_used.name: Post-Traumatic Stress Disorder (PTSD)
source_entries_used.source_pdf: A to Z of Disabilities and Accommodations.pdf
imported_life_limitations: May limit concentration, emotional regulation, reliability, sleep, stress tolerance, communication, social safety, or ability to handle conflict and change.
Can make work/school, appointments, self-care, relationships, and public settings harder even if the disability is invisible.
Stigma and fear of disclosure may create extra barriers to accommodations.
imported_possible_life_improvements_or_s: Disclosure to safe people or formal accommodation processes can open access to flexible schedules, quiet space, written instructions, and supportive check-ins.
Skills, treatment, routines, and validation can slightly improve consistency and reduce shame.
source_entries_used.imported_spoon_energy_notes: Mental load may drain spoons before visible tasks start, especially under stress or masking.
source_entries_used.imported_individual_variability_note: Impact can be mild, moderate, severe, intermittent, progressive, or invisible depending on the person and context.

source_entries_used.name: Acute Stress Disorder
source_entries_used.source_pdf: Alphabetical Listing of Mental Disorders.pdf

source_entries_used.imported_spoon_energy_notes: Variable spoon cost; even short tasks may become expensive when they involve crowds, uncertainty, conflict, trauma cues, or sensory overload.
source_entries_used.imported_individual_variability_note: Impact can be mild, moderate, severe, intermittent, progressive, or invisible depending on the person and context.

source_entries_used.name: Attention Deficit Hyperactivity Disorder
source_entries_used.source_pdf: Alphabetical Listing of Mental Disorders.pdf
imported_life_limitations: Can affect attention, communication, sensory processing, speech/language, learning, coordination, executive functioning, memory, transitions, and social expectations.
May make school/work, forms, appointments, chores, hygiene, crowded places, multitasking, or verbal instructions harder without accommodations.
Mismatch between the person’s needs and the environment can be more disabling than the diagnosis alone.
imported_possible_life_improvements_or_s: Understanding the profile can unlock accommodations, assistive technology, communication supports, routines, special interests, and strengths-based planning.
Clear structure and sensory-respectful environments can slightly improve independence, confidence, and task completion.
External structure, novelty, body-doubling, and reward systems can make tasks more doable and may harness creativity or quick pattern-shifting.
source_entries_used.imported_spoon_energy_notes: Spoon cost often rises with transitions, sensory overload, unclear instructions, masking, or executive-function demands.
source_entries_used.imported_individual_variability_note: Impact can be mild, moderate, severe, intermittent, progressive, or invisible depending on the person and context.

source_entries_used.name: Attention Deficit/Hyperactivity
source_entries_used.source_pdf: A to Z of Disabilities and Accommodations.pdf
imported_life_limitations: May limit communication, executive functioning, sensory tolerance, reading/writing, attention, social navigation, or learning in standard environments.
Can make multitasking, vague instructions, loud spaces, transitions, or rapid deadlines disproportionately exhausting.
The person may be capable but needs a different format, pace, or sensory setup.
imported_possible_life_improvements_or_s: Accommodations can reveal strengths by removing unnecessary barriers in communication, timing, sensory load, or format.
Assistive technology, visual supports, and predictable routines can slightly improve independence and task completion.
source_entries_used.imported_spoon_energy_notes: Spoon cost increases with sensory overload, transitions, masking, and executive-function demands.
source_entries_used.imported_individual_variability_note: Impact can be mild, moderate, severe, intermittent, progressive, or invisible depending on the person and context.

source_entries_used.name: Autism Spectrum Disorder
source_entries_used.source_pdf: Alphabetical Listing of Mental Disorders.pdf
imported_life_limitations: Can affect attention, communication, sensory processing, speech/language, learning, coordination, executive functioning, memory, transitions, and social expectations.
May make school/work, forms, appointments, chores, hygiene, crowded places, multitasking, or verbal instructions harder without accommodations.
Mismatch between the person’s needs and the environment can be more disabling than the diagnosis alone.
imported_possible_life_improvements_or_s: Understanding the profile can unlock accommodations, assistive technology, communication supports, routines, special interests, and strengths-based planning.
Clear structure and sensory-respectful environments can slightly improve independence, confidence, and task completion.
Respecting sensory needs and communication style can let strengths such as deep focus, pattern recognition, honesty, or specialized interests show more easily.
source_entries_used.imported_spoon_energy_notes: Spoon cost often rises with transitions, sensory overload, unclear instructions, masking, or executive-function demands.
source_entries_used.imported_individual_variability_note: Impact can be mild, moderate, severe, intermittent, progressive, or invisible depending on the person and context.

source_entries_used.name: Autism Spectrum
source_entries_used.source_pdf: A to Z of Disabilities and Accommodations.pdf
imported_life_limitations: May limit communication, executive functioning, sensory tolerance, reading/writing, attention, social navigation, or learning in standard environments.
Can make multitasking, vague instructions, loud spaces, transitions, or rapid deadlines disproportionately exhausting.
The person may be capable but needs a different format, pace, or sensory setup.
imported_possible_life_improvements_or_s: Accommodations can reveal strengths by removing unnecessary barriers in communication, timing, sensory load, or format.
Assistive technology, visual supports, and predictable routines can slightly improve independence and task completion.
source_entries_used.imported_spoon_energy_notes: Spoon cost increases with sensory overload, transitions, masking, and executive-function demands.
source_entries_used.imported_individual_variability_note: Impact can be mild, moderate, severe, intermittent, progressive, or invisible depending on the person and context.

source_entries_used.name: Gender Dysphoria
source_entries_used.source_pdf: Alphabetical Listing of Mental Disorders.pdf
imported_life_limitations: Can affect intimacy, body safety, pain, consent communication, relationships, shame, medical care, or gender-related distress depending on the condition.
May make closeness, touch, dating, pelvic/sexual health care, or identity expression emotionally and physically complicated.
Stigma can prevent people from seeking support.
Can be intensified by misgendering, unsafe environments, barriers to gender-affirming care, or inability to express identity safely.
imported_possible_life_improvements_or_s: Recognition can support trauma-informed care, pelvic/sexual health treatment, safer communication, and identity-affirming support when relevant.
Clear consent and pacing can slightly improve safety, trust, and comfort.
Affirming names/pronouns, privacy, and safe identity expression can improve comfort and reduce distress.
source_entries_used.imported_spoon_energy_notes: Spoon cost depends on pain, dysphoria, shame, fear, relational safety, and sensory needs.
source_entries_used.imported_individual_variability_note: Impact can be mild, moderate, severe, intermittent, progressive, or invisible depending on the person and context.

source_entries_used.name: Bereavement
source_entries_used.source_pdf: Alphabetical Listing of Mental Disorders.pdf
imported_life_limitations: Can affect energy, motivation, sleep, appetite, self-care, emotional regulation, follow-through, pleasure, and hope.
May make chores, hygiene, work/school, communication, socializing, and long-term planning feel heavy or inconsistent.
Mood swings or episodes can disrupt routines and relationships even when the person is trying hard.
imported_possible_life_improvements_or_s: A clear label can help the person track cycles, ask for support earlier, and use treatment/routine strategies before symptoms snowball.
Mood tracking and compassionate pacing can slightly improve predictability, self-advocacy, and recovery time after difficult days.
Grief recognition can allow community care, rituals, and reduced expectations while the person mourns.
source_entries_used.imported_spoon_energy_notes: Spoon availability may change by episode/day; tiny tasks may need to be split into steps with rest and reinforcement.
source_entries_used.imported_individual_variability_note: Impact can be mild, moderate, severe, intermittent, progressive, or invisible depending on the person and context.

source_entries_used.name: Narcolepsy
source_entries_used.source_pdf: Alphabetical Listing of Mental Disorders.pdf
imported_life_limitations: Can reduce daytime energy, focus, mood stability, pain tolerance, reaction time, immune resilience, and ability to keep schedules.
May limit driving, work/school consistency, social life, self-care, and safety when sleep is disrupted or attacks occur.
Poor sleep can worsen other physical and mental health conditions.
imported_possible_life_improvements_or_s: Naming the sleep pattern can support sleep hygiene, medical evaluation, schedule accommodations, and safety planning.
Predictable rest windows and lower morning demands can slightly improve daily reliability.
source_entries_used.imported_spoon_energy_notes: Often reduces starting spoon count for the whole day; sleep loss makes every later task cost more.
source_entries_used.imported_individual_variability_note: Impact can be mild, moderate, severe, intermittent, progressive, or invisible depending on the person and context.

imported_life_limitations: May limit memory, coordination, seizure safety, movement, balance, speech, stamina, pain control, cognition, or independence.
Can make driving, machinery, stairs, deadlines, public settings, and complex tasks unsafe or inconsistent during flares/episodes.
Symptoms may be episodic or progressive, requiring flexible planning.
imported_possible_life_improvements_or_s: Safety plans, assistive technology, therapy, routines, and flexible expectations can improve predictability and reduce risk.
Recognizing triggers/episodes can slightly improve planning and confidence.
source_entries_used.imported_spoon_energy_notes: Spoon cost varies sharply with episodes, fatigue, medication effects, and sensory/cognitive load.
source_entries_used.imported_individual_variability_note: Impact can be mild, moderate, severe, intermittent, progressive, or invisible depending on the person and context.

imported_possible_life_improvements_or_s: Naming the limitation can make accommodation conversations more practical because support can focus on the actual barrier instead of only the diagnosis.
Assistive technology, altered timing, task redesign, or environmental changes can slightly improve independence and reduce exhaustion.
source_entries_used.imported_spoon_energy_notes: Spoon cost rises when the task repeatedly demands this function without breaks or tools.
source_entries_used.imported_individual_variability_note: Impact can be mild, moderate, severe, intermittent, progressive, or invisible depending on the person and context.

source_entries_used.name: Spoon Theory / limited daily energy
source_entries_used.source_pdf: What Is Spoon Theory__ Eric K. Fanaee, MD_ Board Certified in Pain Medicine and Anesthesiology.pdf
imported_life_limitations: Can limit how many tasks, appointments, chores, social plans, and care activities fit into a day.
Forces tradeoffs: doing one task may mean losing capacity for another, even when the person wants to do both.
Can make invisible effort hard for others to understand.
imported_possible_life_improvements_or_s: Provides a simple shared language for boundaries, pacing, task prioritization, and compassion.
Can slightly improve planning by making energy visible and countable.
source_entries_used.imported_spoon_energy_notes: Every task should have an estimated spoon cost and a recovery option.
source_entries_used.imported_individual_variability_note: Impact can be mild, moderate, severe, intermittent, progressive, or invisible depending on the person and context.

source_entries_used.name: Day-to-day variability
source_entries_used.source_pdf: What Is Spoon Theory__ Eric K. Fanaee, MD_ Board Certified in Pain Medicine and Anesthesiology.pdf
imported_life_limitations: Makes future planning difficult because tomorrow’s capacity may not match today’s.
Can cause guilt or conflict when commitments must change.
imported_possible_life_improvements_or_s: Encourages flexible planning, backup options, and permission to reschedule without shame.
source_entries_used.imported_spoon_energy_notes: Use ranges rather than fixed costs; include flare/crash mode.
source_entries_used.imported_individual_variability_note: Impact can be mild, moderate, severe, intermittent, progressive, or invisible depending on the person and context.

source_entries_used.name: Invisible illness visibility
source_entries_used.source_pdf: What Is Spoon Theory__ Eric K. Fanaee, MD_ Board Certified in Pain Medicine and Anesthesiology.pdf
imported_life_limitations: Can cause disbelief, pressure to overperform, lack of accommodations, and social misunderstanding.
May make people feel obligated to explain private health details.
imported_possible_life_improvements_or_s: A shared term such as “spoonie” can help people find community and explain needs quickly if they choose.
imported_support_or_accommodation_ideas: avoid judging how energy is spent
source_entries_used.imported_spoon_energy_notes: Social invalidation itself costs spoons.
source_entries_used.imported_individual_variability_note: Impact can be mild, moderate, severe, intermittent, progressive, or invisible depending on the person and context.

source_entries_used.name: Boundary communication
source_entries_used.source_pdf: What Is Spoon Theory__ Eric K. Fanaee, MD_ Board Certified in Pain Medicine and Anesthesiology.pdf
imported_life_limitations: People may still judge how someone spends limited energy or expect justification.
The person may need to protect energy by saying no.
imported_possible_life_improvements_or_s: Can make it easier to ask for help, explain cancellations, and honor needs without over-explaining.
source_entries_used.imported_spoon_energy_notes: Boundary-setting may cost spoons at first but can prevent larger crashes later.
source_entries_used.imported_individual_variability_note: Impact can be mild, moderate, severe, intermittent, progressive, or invisible depending on the person and context.

source_entries_used.name: Community and peer support
source_entries_used.source_pdf: What Is Spoon Theory__ Eric K. Fanaee, MD_ Board Certified in Pain Medicine and Anesthesiology.pdf
imported_life_limitations: Without community, chronic illness/disability can feel isolating and misunderstood.
Peer spaces can still be tiring if comparison or unsolicited advice becomes overwhelming.
imported_possible_life_improvements_or_s: Peer support can offer emotional support, practical tips, identity validation, and reduced isolation.
source_entries_used.imported_spoon_energy_notes: Community can return spoons emotionally, but only when it is safe and not demanding.
source_entries_used.imported_individual_variability_note: Impact can be mild, moderate, severe, intermittent, progressive, or invisible depending on the person and context.

source_entries_used.name: Use of Mobility Aids
source_entries_used.source_pdf: A to Z of Disabilities and Accommodations.pdf
imported_life_limitations: Can limit the specific daily/work function named here: Use of Mobility Aids.
May make tasks slower, less safe, more painful, more tiring, or less reliable unless the environment is adjusted.
Can interact with other conditions, so a person may need supports that address multiple limitations at once.
imported_possible_life_improvements_or_s: Naming the limitation can make accommodation conversations more practical because support can focus on the actual barrier instead of only the diagnosis.
Assistive technology, altered timing, task redesign, or environmental changes can slightly improve independence and reduce exhaustion.
source_entries_used.imported_spoon_energy_notes: Spoon cost rises when the task repeatedly demands this function without breaks or tools.
source_entries_used.imported_individual_variability_note: Impact can be mild, moderate, severe, intermittent, progressive, or invisible depending on the person and context.

source_entries_used.name: Personality Disorder
source_entries_used.source_pdf: A to Z of Disabilities and Accommodations.pdf
imported_life_limitations: May limit concentration, emotional regulation, reliability, sleep, stress tolerance, communication, social safety, or ability to handle conflict and change.
Can make work/school, appointments, self-care, relationships, and public settings harder even if the disability is invisible.
Stigma and fear of disclosure may create extra barriers to accommodations.
imported_possible_life_improvements_or_s: Disclosure to safe people or formal accommodation processes can open access to flexible schedules, quiet space, written instructions, and supportive check-ins.
Skills, treatment, routines, and validation can slightly improve consistency and reduce shame.
source_entries_used.imported_spoon_energy_notes: Mental load may drain spoons before visible tasks start, especially under stress or masking.
source_entries_used.imported_individual_variability_note: Impact can be mild, moderate, severe, intermittent, progressive, or invisible depending on the person and context.

source_entries_used.name: Mental Health Conditions
source_entries_used.source_pdf: A to Z of Disabilities and Accommodations.pdf
imported_life_limitations: May limit concentration, emotional regulation, reliability, sleep, stress tolerance, communication, social safety, or ability to handle conflict and change.
Can make work/school, appointments, self-care, relationships, and public settings harder even if the disability is invisible.
Stigma and fear of disclosure may create extra barriers to accommodations.
imported_possible_life_improvements_or_s: Disclosure to safe people or formal accommodation processes can open access to flexible schedules, quiet space, written instructions, and supportive check-ins.
Skills, treatment, routines, and validation can slightly improve consistency and reduce shame.
source_entries_used.imported_spoon_energy_notes: Mental load may drain spoons before visible tasks start, especially under stress or masking.
source_entries_used.imported_individual_variability_note: Impact can be mild, moderate, severe, intermittent, progressive, or invisible depending on the person and context.

source_entries_used.name: Leg Impairment
source_entries_used.source_pdf: A to Z of Disabilities and Accommodations.pdf
imported_life_limitations: May limit walking, standing, lifting, reaching, transfers, balance, speed, endurance, pain control, and access to inaccessible spaces.
Can make errands, bathing, cooking, transportation, cleaning, work sites, and social activities dependent on ramps, seating, rest, or assistance.
Fall risk, pain flares, and inaccessible buildings can reduce independence even when the person has strong coping skills.
imported_possible_life_improvements_or_s: Mobility aids, ergonomic tools, accessible layouts, and pacing can improve safety, range, and dignity.
Planning around energy, pain, and access can slightly increase participation and reduce injury.
source_entries_used.imported_spoon_energy_notes: Physical spoon cost rises with distance, stairs, carrying, transfers, and repeated movements.
source_entries_used.imported_individual_variability_note: Impact can be mild, moderate, severe, intermittent, progressive, or invisible depending on the person and context.

source_entries_used.name: Skin Conditions
source_entries_used.source_pdf: A to Z of Disabilities and Accommodations.pdf
imported_life_limitations: May limit stamina, pain tolerance, immune safety, digestion, breathing, sleep, temperature tolerance, medical scheduling, or physical reliability.
Can make work/school, errands, self-care, food planning, socializing, and travel harder due to symptoms, flares, treatment, or environmental triggers.
Invisible symptoms may be misunderstood, causing pressure to overexert.
imported_possible_life_improvements_or_s: Medical recognition and accommodations can support safer pacing, trigger reduction, treatment access, and clearer expectations.
Adaptive routines can slightly improve autonomy and reduce flares or crashes.
source_entries_used.imported_spoon_energy_notes: Spoon cost often fluctuates with flares, treatment side effects, pain, fatigue, weather, food, sleep, and stress.
source_entries_used.imported_individual_variability_note: Impact can be mild, moderate, severe, intermittent, progressive, or invisible depending on the person and context.

source_entries_used.name: Neurodiversity
source_entries_used.source_pdf: A to Z of Disabilities and Accommodations.pdf
imported_life_limitations: May limit communication, executive functioning, sensory tolerance, reading/writing, attention, social navigation, or learning in standard environments.
Can make multitasking, vague instructions, loud spaces, transitions, or rapid deadlines disproportionately exhausting.
The person may be capable but needs a different format, pace, or sensory setup.
imported_possible_life_improvements_or_s: Accommodations can reveal strengths by removing unnecessary barriers in communication, timing, sensory load, or format.
Assistive technology, visual supports, and predictable routines can slightly improve independence and task completion.
source_entries_used.imported_spoon_energy_notes: Spoon cost increases with sensory overload, transitions, masking, and executive-function demands.
source_entries_used.imported_individual_variability_note: Impact can be mild, moderate, severe, intermittent, progressive, or invisible depending on the person and context.

source_entries_used.name: Generalized Anxiety Disorder
source_entries_used.source_pdf: Alphabetical Listing of Mental Disorders.pdf
imported_life_limitations: Can shrink the person’s world through avoidance, panic, hypervigilance, intrusive worry, and fear of places/situations where escape or safety feels uncertain.
May limit appointments, errands, work/school, social connection, sleep, concentration, travel, conflict resolution, and asking for help.
The person may appear “fine” while spending a large amount of internal energy monitoring threat or preventing a panic/trauma response.
imported_possible_life_improvements_or_s: Recognizing the condition can support grounding skills, exposure at a tolerable pace, trauma-informed care, and clearer boundaries.
Predictable routines, safety plans, and permission to pause can slightly improve autonomy and reduce shame.
source_entries_used.imported_spoon_energy_notes: Variable spoon cost; even short tasks may become expensive when they involve crowds, uncertainty, conflict, trauma cues, or sensory overload.
source_entries_used.imported_individual_variability_note: Impact can be mild, moderate, severe, intermittent, progressive, or invisible depending on the person and context.

source_entries_used.name: Learning Disability
source_entries_used.source_pdf: A to Z of Disabilities and Accommodations.pdf
imported_life_limitations: May limit communication, executive functioning, sensory tolerance, reading/writing, attention, social navigation, or learning in standard environments.
Can make multitasking, vague instructions, loud spaces, transitions, or rapid deadlines disproportionately exhausting.
The person may be capable but needs a different format, pace, or sensory setup.
imported_possible_life_improvements_or_s: Accommodations can reveal strengths by removing unnecessary barriers in communication, timing, sensory load, or format.
Assistive technology, visual supports, and predictable routines can slightly improve independence and task completion.
source_entries_used.imported_spoon_energy_notes: Spoon cost increases with sensory overload, transitions, masking, and executive-function demands.
source_entries_used.imported_individual_variability_note: Impact can be mild, moderate, severe, intermittent, progressive, or invisible depending on the person and context.
